# GitHub Copilot Instructions

## Project Purpose

This is a root-module-only Terraform configuration for importing an existing HCP Terraform or Terraform Enterprise organization (its teams, projects, and workspaces) into Terraform state. It is **not** a reusable module — import blocks are only valid in root declarations.

## Technology Versions

- Terraform: `~> 1.8`
- Provider `hashicorp/tfe`: `~> 0.57.0`
- Provider `devops-rob/terracurl`: `1.2.1`

Never suggest features or syntax beyond these versions.

## Commands

```sh
make init      # terraform init
make plan      # terraform plan
make apply     # terraform init + plan + apply
make destroy   # terraform apply -destroy
make fmt       # terraform fmt && tflint
make docs      # terraform-docs markdown table --output-file README.md .
```

Run a targeted plan: `terraform plan -var import_workspaces="data"`

Update README docs: `terraform-docs markdown table --output-file README.md .` (requires [terraform-docs](https://terraform-docs.io/)).
Lint: `terraform fmt && tflint` (requires [tflint](https://github.com/terraform-linters/tflint)).

## Architecture: Three-Phase Import Pattern

Every resource collection (`organization`, `teams`, `projects`, `workspaces`) follows the same three-phase lifecycle controlled by a string-enum variable:

| Variable value | Effect |
|---|---|
| `"false"` | No data sources or resources active; outputs are empty/null |
| `"data"` | Data sources and terracurl API calls active; outputs populated for inspection. No resources managed. |
| `"true"` | Import blocks and resources active; requires the corresponding `*_import_map` variable to be populated |

The workflow is always: `false` → `data` (capture output) → `true` (import). **Stepping back from `true` to `data` or `false` is destructive.**

Each resource file (e.g. `workspaces.tf`) contains all of: data sources, terracurl API call, locals, outputs, import block, and resource — co-located in one file per resource collection.

## Key Conventions

### `for_each` guard pattern
All data sources, import blocks, and resources are gated with `for_each` using the string-enum variables:
```hcl
for_each = contains(["data", "true"], var.import_workspaces) ? { "data" = "true" } : {}
```
The sentinel key `"data"` is always used as the single map entry. Never use `count`.

### terracurl → locals → output pipeline
Raw API responses from `terracurl_request` are always decoded in a `locals` block via `jsondecode()`, then referenced in outputs. Never output the raw response string directly.

```hcl
locals {
  terracurl_workspaces_response = var.import_workspaces == "data" ? jsondecode(data.terracurl_request.workspaces["data"].response) : null
}
```

### Import map convention
Import maps fed into `import` blocks and `resource` blocks always share the same `for_each` expression. The `import` block must immediately precede the matching `resource` block.

```hcl
import {
  for_each = var.import_workspaces == "true" && var.workspaces_import_map != null ? var.workspaces_import_map : {}
  to       = tfe_workspace.this[each.key]
  id       = each.value
}
resource "tfe_workspace" "this" {
  for_each = var.import_workspaces == "true" && var.workspaces_import_map != null ? var.workspaces_import_map : {}
  ...
}
```

### Variable naming
- Control variables: `import_<resource>` (string enum `"true"/"data"/"false"`)
- Map inputs: `<resource>_import_map` (type `map(any)`)
- Outputs for feeding back into map inputs: `<resource>_import_map`
- Outputs for raw API data: `terracurl_data_<resource>_response`

### Sensitive credentials
`tfc_org_token` is marked `sensitive = true`. Auth is always passed as a Bearer token header in terracurl requests. The recommended pattern is:
```sh
export TFE_TOKEN="..."
export TF_VAR_tfc_org_token=$TFE_TOKEN
```

### API pagination
The terracurl requests use default TFE API pagination (20 items). If more than 20 items exist, use a `*_override.tf` file to extend the requests. Don't modify the original resource files.

### Override pattern
To customise any resource file without modifying it: `cp ./workspaces.tf workspaces_override.tf` (via `make terraform-override file=workspaces.tf`).

### Variable descriptions
Follow this format in `variables.tf`:
```
(Optional ["true", "data", "false"]) Description of what this controls
```
or
```
(Optional Map) Description including example value
```

### Section separators in variables.tf
Group variables by resource collection using comment separators:
```hcl
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# <Resource Collection>
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

## README

README.md is auto-generated by `terraform-docs` between `<!-- BEGIN_TF_DOCS -->` and `<!-- END_TF_DOCS -->` markers. Do not manually edit that section — run `make docs` instead.
